title: Ramp Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Ramp** is a corporate-card and spend-management platform exposing a REST API under `https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1`. This connector — `RampConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "ramp"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Ramp:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users`, `/users/deferred` | List, get, invite |
| Cards | `/cards` | List, get |
| Transactions | `/transactions` | List, get |
| Departments | `/departments` | List |
| Locations | `/locations` | List |
| Reimbursements | `/reimbursements` | List |
| Bills | `/bills` | List |
| Vendors | `/vendors` | List |
| Limits | `/limits` | List |
| Memos | `/memos` | List, get |

The connector normalises users + transactions into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), refreshes the OAuth2 access_token on 401 (exactly once per request), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::RampHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Ramp API credentials

1. Sign in to https://app.ramp.com.
2. Open **Settings → Developer → API → Create new app**.
3. Grant the scopes you need (`users:read`, `users:write`, `cards:read`, `transactions:read`, `reimbursements:read`, `bills:read`, `vendors:read`, `departments:read`, `locations:read`, `limits:read`, `memos:read`).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — Ramp shows the secret only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Ramp**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` | OAuth2 client_id from step 4 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | OAuth2 client_secret from step 4 |
| `scopes` (optional) | Override default scope list |
| `base_url` (optional) | Override `https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1` |
| `token_url` (optional) | Override `https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are present and mints a probe access_token. On 401 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On success → `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 user as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx/network → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Ramp Developer API uses **OAuth2 client_credentials** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`). No user-facing auth-code dance, no refresh token — the client_id+client_secret are exchanged directly for an access_token.

### Credential model

- `client_id` and `client_secret` form the HTTP Basic credential on the `/token` request.
- Token grant body: `grant_type=client_credentials` (+ optional `scope=<space-separated>`).
- The access_token is cached in memory inside `RampHTTPClient` with a 60 s safety margin before expiry; re-minted on first call / expiry.
- A 401 from the API invalidates the cached token and triggers exactly one fresh mint + retry. A second 401 surfaces as `RampAuthError`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Probes the token endpoint. Returns `AUTHENTICATED+HEALTHY` on success. |
| `authorize(...)` | Runs the client_credentials grant and returns a `TokenInfo` (carries the access_token, expires_at, scopes). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users?page_size=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Handled inside `RampHTTPClient._get_token()`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound API request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:   application/json
Accept:         application/json
Idempotency-Key: <uuid>   ← POST mutations only
```

Token-mint request:
```
POST https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token
Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 client_id |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 client_secret |
| `scopes` | string | no | `self.scopes` | Space-separated scope list |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1`) | Sandbox / regional override |
| `token_url` | string | no | `self.token_url` (default `https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token`) | Sandbox / regional override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_RAMP_BASE = "https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1"
_RAMP_TOKEN_URL = "https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `RampConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::RampHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`, mint a probe access_token via the OAuth2 client_credentials grant. Returns `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED` on success.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Runs the client_credentials grant and returns a TokenInfo. No auth-code is used.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 user via `GET /users?page_size=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Ramp users + transactions → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async list_users(*, department_id=None, location_id=None, role=None, start=None, page_size=50) -> Dict`
`GET /users?department_id=&location_id=&role=&start=&page_size=`. Cursor pagination via `start`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{user_id}`.

### `async invite_user(first_name, last_name, email, role="BUSINESS_USER", department_id=None, location_id=None, idempotency_key=None) -> Dict`
`POST /users/deferred` with the user payload. Pass `idempotency_key` to safely retry the invite.

children.title: Cards
children.content:
### `async list_cards(*, user_id=None, start=None, page_size=50, is_physical=None) -> Dict`
`GET /cards`. `is_physical` is encoded as `"true"` / `"false"`.

### `async get_card(card_id) -> Dict`
`GET /cards/{card_id}`.

children.title: Transactions
children.content:
### `async list_transactions(*, start=None, end=None, sk_category_id=None, merchant_id=None, page_size=50) -> Dict`
`GET /transactions`. `start`/`end` are ISO-8601 timestamps and map to `from_date` / `to_date` query params.

### `async get_transaction(transaction_id) -> Dict`
`GET /transactions/{transaction_id}`.

children.title: Departments · Locations · Vendors · Bills · Reimbursements · Limits
children.content:
### `async list_departments(*, start=None, page_size=50) -> Dict`
`GET /departments`.

### `async list_locations(*, start=None, page_size=50) -> Dict`
`GET /locations`.

### `async list_reimbursements(*, start=None, page_size=50, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /reimbursements`.

### `async list_bills(*, page_size=50, start=None) -> Dict`
`GET /bills`.

### `async list_vendors(*, page_size=50, start=None) -> Dict`
`GET /vendors`.

### `async list_limits(*, page_size=50, start=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /limits`.

children.title: Memos
children.content:
### `async list_memos(*, page_size=50, start=None) -> Dict`
`GET /memos`.

### `async get_memo(memo_id) -> Dict`
`GET /memos/{memo_id}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
RampError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── RampAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── RampBadRequestError                # 400
├── RampNotFoundError                  # 404
├── RampConflictError                  # 409
├── RampRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
└── RampServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
RampNetworkError = RampServerError     # legacy name from older code
RampNotFound     = RampNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | Invalidate cached token, mint fresh, retry once. A second 401 raises `RampAuthError`. |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s); honours `Retry-After` header; retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `RampNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)  ← via install probe
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            ← logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Ramp shows the secret only once at creation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401 from Ramp `/token` — credentials wrong | Verify the client_id matches the secret. Rotate the secret in Ramp Dashboard → Developer → API. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | Two consecutive 401s from `/users` even after token refresh | The token grant succeeded but the API still rejects — usually missing scopes. Edit the Ramp app and grant `users:read`. |
| `ramp.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Ramp per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff and honours `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_user(uid)` / `get_card(cid)` / `get_transaction(tid)` | Stale id, or resource lives in a different Ramp account | Verify the id in the Ramp dashboard. The connector only sees the account that owns the OAuth2 app. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'RampNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `RampNetworkError = RampServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Token mints repeatedly inside a single batch | `expires_in` in the token response is very small (sandbox sometimes returns 60) | Inspect the token response; consider extending `_token_expires_at` margin if your account has unusually short TTLs. |
